headline: Educators converge on harnesses, orchestration, and routing that treat coding agents as interchangeable workers governed by identity and cost.
body: The recommended channels center on agent orchestration: Cole Medin demos Omnigent, an open-source meta-harness that routes tasks across Claude Code, Codex, and Pi with code review and human-in-the-loop governance [3], while Cyril Imhof walks through Claude Fable 5 harness engineering and loops alongside open versus closed source tradeoffs [1]. IBM Technology frames the supporting infrastructure: smart routing that directs requests across models for cost and trust rather than defaulting to the largest [6], Kagenti's identity-based zero-trust security and delegation chains for multi-agent systems [2], and the question of whether AI agents are ready to manage passwords and credentials [0]. On the model and tooling side, AICodeKing covers MiniMax M3 with million-token context and Anthropic and OpenAI endpoint compatibility [8], AI Revolution covers OpenRouter Fusion ensembling multiple models at roughly half the cost [10], and AWS shows Claude Code on Bedrock and an Agent Toolkit that supplies verified procedures and guardrails [11][13].
